papers.title: E-TTS: A New Embodied Test-Time Scaling Framework for Robotic Manipulation
papers.abstract_snippet: Recently, a few works have made early attempts to study test-time scaling for embodied tasks. However, two major challenges remain unsolved: (1) reasoning can effectively improve the performance of the policy, but its scaling mechanism has seldom been studied; (2) historical information is essential, as embodied tasks are inherently long-horizon and sequential, making sole reliance on current observations for action scaling inadequate due to the lack of historical context utilization. To address
papers.reason: 提出测试时缩放框架，通过推理机制和历史信息利用提升策略性能。为VLA部署提供新的推理优化思路，可直接应用于现有模型以提升长尾任务表现。

papers.title: RMTL: Reinforced Micro-task Learning for Long-Horizon Manipulation with VLM Rewards
papers.authors: Anıl Can Ateş, Orhan Kahraman, Cihan Topal
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2606.26175v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) for robotic manipulation often requires manually designing a dense reward function, which is difficult to tune and often fragile, or learning a reward from human demonstrations or preferences, which can be expensive. A recent line of work uses pretrained vision-language models (VLMs) as zero-shot reward models, replacing these costs with a single text prompt. However, we argue that a single global prompt is too coarse fo
papers.reason: 利用VLM作为奖励函数解决长程操作RL稀疏奖励问题，结合微任务学习分解复杂任务。提供了一种无需密集人工标注的RL训练范式，适用于长程操作场景。

papers.title: LiMoDE: Rethinking Lifelong Robot Manipulation from a Mixture-of-Dynamic-Experts Perspective
papers.authors: Zhihao Gu, Lin Wang
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2606.26183v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Building a generalist robot that can leverage prior knowledge for continuous task adaptation remains a significant challenge. Previous works alleviate the catastrophic forgetting problem by parameter-efficient fine-tuning for single-task adaptation. However, they fail to extract reusable skills and model the interaction with other skills effectively. Recent works try to address these issues by learning prompts. papers.reason: 从动态专家混合视角研究终身机器人操作以缓解灾难性遗忘。方法相邻但缺乏明确的VLA架构集成路径，且主要关注参数高效微调，属于增量改进。

papers.reason: 专注于移动机器人的导航避障与人群模拟，不涉及机械臂操作或VLA核心能力，属于自动驾驶/移动导航领域。

papers.title: WatchAct: A Benchmark for Behavior-Grounded Robot Manipulation
papers.authors: Baiqi Li, Ce Zhang, Yu Fang, Yue Yang, Shangzhe Li, Mingyu Ding
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2606.26443v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: A robot working alongside people must reason about what they have done, in what order, and with what intent. Video carries the spatial layouts, object histories, and gestures that language leaves underspecified, yet today's manipulation benchmarks pair an instruction with a single current image, offering no way to evaluate reasoning over observed human behavior. papers.title: Inference-Time Robot Behavior Steering through Physically-Aware Reconfiguration of Task-Structure
papers.authors: Yiyuan Pan, Hanjiang Hu, Shangtao Li, Xusheng Luo, Changliu Liu
papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2606.26588v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: A central challenge in deploying learned robot policies is inference-time behavior steering: redirecting a policy at test time to satisfy user preferences not anticipated during training, without retraining. papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2606.26603v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Handheld data collection systems, such as the Universal Manipulation Interface (UMI), enable scalable data collection across diverse environments but only capture observed actions rather than the desired actions executed by a robot controller. papers.authors: Mingyang Lyu, Yinqian Sun, Yiyang Jia, Sicheng Shen, Moquan Sha, Huangrui Li

papers.title: PhysReflect-VLA: Physical Feasibility and Self-Reflective Regulation for Reliable Vision-Language-Action Policies

papers.title: RouterVLA: Turning Smoke Tests into Supervision for Heterogeneous VLA Selection

papers.title: Hallucination in World Models is Predictable and Preventable

papers.abstract_snippet: arXiv:2606.26663v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: World Action Models (WAMs) generate actions together with predicted futures, offering a powerful interface for robot decision making.
